displayTitle: Inspect the Substrate, Then the Coating

beats.narration: Coating inspection is staged visual testing. You inspect the same part twice: bare substrate before paint, and the finished film after.

payload.title: COATING INSPECTION
payload.sub: Two-stage visual testing: the bare part, then the finished film.

beats.narration: Stage one is the substrate. Before the first coat, verify cleanliness and blast profile against the written and photographic standards your procedure invokes.
Here's the catch — paint hides discontinuities and seals in contamination. A coat over rust isn't protection; it's a failure on a delay timer.

payload.heading: Paint hides what you didn't fix
payload.note: COAT OVER RUST = FAILURE ON A DELAY TIMER

beats.narration: Stage two is the coating. Check each coat for pinholes, then the final film for runs, sags, blisters, and holidays — breaks exposing bare substrate.
The coating you can see is only half the inspection. The surface you can no longer see is the other half.

payload.sub: The surface under the coating is the other half.
